CREATE TABLE test_map_2 (id INTEGER, m MAP<VARCHAR, INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_map_2 VALUES (1, MAP {'key1': 10}), (2, MAP {'key2': 20, 'key3': 30});
SELECT id, map_contains_entry(m, 'key1', 10) FROM test_map_2 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, map_contains_entry(m, 'key3', 30) FROM test_map_2 WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, map_contains_entry(m, 'key2', 20) FROM test_map_2 WHERE id IN (1, 2);
SELECT CASE WHEN map_contains_entry(m, 'key1', 10) THEN 'Exists' ELSE 'Does not exist' END AS result FROM test_map_2;
WITH map_data AS (SELECT m FROM test_map_2 WHERE id = 2) SELECT map_contains_entry(m, 'key2', 20) FROM map_data;
SELECT map_contains_entry(m, 'type', 'Database') AND map_contains_entry(m, 'name', 'DuckDB') FROM test_map_3;
